examples.title: 4-to-1 Multiplexer Simulation
examples.description: Python code to simulate a 4-to-1 multiplexer
examples.code:
def mux_4to1(I, S):
    # I: list of 4 inputs [I0, I1, I2, I3]
    # S: list of 2 select bits [S0, S1]
    index = (S[0] << 1) | S[1]  # Convert to 0-3
    return I[index]

# Test
inputs = [0, 1, 0, 1]
select = [1, 0]  # Select I2 (binary 10 = 2)
print(f"Output: {mux_4to1(inputs, select)}")  # Output: 0
examples.explanation: This code simulates a 4-to-1 multiplexer by using select lines as binary index to select one of four inputs.

examples.title: D Flip-Flop Behavioral Model
examples.description: C code modeling D flip-flop behavior with positive edge clock
examples.explanation: This models a D flip-flop that transfers D to Q only on positive clock transition (rising edge).

examples.title: 3-bit Asynchronous (Ripple) Counter
examples.description: Python simulation of 3-bit ripple counter behavior
examples.code:
def ripple_counter(clock_pulses):
    # Simulate 3-bit ripple counter (counts 0-7)
    Q0, Q1, Q2 = 0, 0, 0  # Three flip-flop outputs
    count_history = []
    
    for i in range(clock_pulses):
        # Toggle Q0 on every clock (LSB)
        Q0 = not Q0
        # Toggle Q1 when Q0 goes from 1 to 0
        if not Q0:
            Q1 = not Q1
        # Toggle Q2 when Q1 goes from 1 to 0
        if not Q1 and Q0:  # Simplified
            Q2 = not Q2
        
        count = (Q2 << 2) | (Q1 << 1) | Q0
        count_history.append(count)
    
    return count_history

# Simulate 8 clock pulses
print(ripple_counter(8))  # Output: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 0]
examples.explanation: This simulates a ripple counter where each flip-flop is clocked by the output of the previous one, causing cumulative delay.

examples.title: Full Adder Implementation
examples.description: Java implementation of a full adder with carry logic
examples.explanation: Full adder adds three 1-bit inputs. Sum is XOR of all three bits. Carry is generated when at least two inputs are 1.
